methodology.primary: Mainstream-only percentages are my own estimate after reading the requested prediction pieces and then adjusting for late guild/precursor results.
methodology.secondary: With-Polymarket percentages are a 70/30 blend of my mainstream estimate and Polymarket's top-line market price for the category leader.
methodology.note: Deadline and Variety influenced the model mostly through indexed previews/snippets because their pages were only partially accessible in my browsing environment.

nominees: Leonardo DiCaprio - One Battle after Another
ballot.why: Variety's top-line forecast and Vanity Fair both land on Michael B. Jordan, while Deadline's indexed snippet tied the race to his late-run wins. Timothée Chalamet is still live because he took both the Globe and Critics Choice.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Variety snippet; Deadline snippet; Actor Awards; Golden Globes; Critics Choice

nominees: Benicio Del Toro - One Battle after Another
Sean Penn - One Battle after Another
ballot.mainstream_pick: Sean Penn - One Battle after Another
ballot.combined_pick: Sean Penn - One Battle after Another
ballot.challenger: Stellan Skarsgård - Sentimental Value / Delroy Lindo - Sinners
ballot.why: Sean Penn is the cleanest consensus across the mainstream prediction pieces I could verify, and BAFTA also went his way. Delroy Lindo is the upside upset if Sinners starts running hot all night.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Deadline snippet; BAFTA; Polymarket

nominees: Rose Byrne - If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
ballot.challenger: Rose Byrne - If I Had Legs I'd Kick You
ballot.why: This is the closest thing to a free square. Vanity Fair and The New Yorker both call it the easiest acting race, and the New York Times Daily transcript frames Buckley as the consensus winner. She also has Globe, Critics Choice, and BAFTA support.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; NYT The Daily transcript; Golden Globes; Critics Choice; BAFTA

nominees: Teyana Taylor - One Battle after Another
ballot.challenger: Teyana Taylor - One Battle after Another
ballot.why: This is the messiest major-category call. Amy Madigan won Critics Choice and the Actor Awards, Teyana Taylor won the Globe and gets Vanity Fair's nod, and Wunmi Mosaku took BAFTA. I still lean Amy because the late industry prizes matter most, but this is absolutely the ballot's biggest sweat.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Critics Choice; Actor Awards; Golden Globes; BAFTA; Polymarket

nominees: Little Amélie or the Character of Rain
ballot.why: KPop Demon Hunters has the strongest animation-season profile by far: Annie sweep, Globe win, and a Vanity Fair prediction. The market agrees it's basically the field's animation lock.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Annie Awards; Golden Globes; Polymarket

ballot.why: Vanity Fair and Polymarket both edge toward Butterfly, but short categories are chaotic by nature, so I would not overstate confidence here.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Polymarket

ballot.why: Since this is the first year for casting, I leaned heavily on the Artios result and the overall broad-ensemble read. Sinners fits both and gets Vanity Fair's vote.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Artios; Polymarket

ballot.why: Vanity Fair flagged this as neck-and-neck, but the late precursors broke toward One Battle after Another: BAFTA cinematography and ASC both went there. That's enough to override earlier Sinners momentum.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; BAFTA; ASC; Polymarket

ballot.why: This is the night's central fight. One Battle after Another has PGA, DGA, BAFTA Best Film, and both Vanity Fair and The New Yorker leaning its way. Sinners has the stronger passion narrative and the Actor Awards ensemble win, so I would keep it as the only serious upset path.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Variety snippet; Deadline snippet; PGA; DGA; BAFTA; Actor Awards; Polymarket

ballot.why: Frankenstein dominated the major costume indicators and gets Vanity Fair's pick as well. This is one of the sturdier craft calls on the board.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; BAFTA; Polymarket

nominees: One Battle after Another - Paul Thomas Anderson
ballot.mainstream_pick: One Battle after Another - Paul Thomas Anderson
ballot.combined_pick: One Battle after Another - Paul Thomas Anderson
ballot.why: This looks close to done. Anderson won DGA and BAFTA, and both Vanity Fair and The New Yorker say this is where he finally gets his directing Oscar.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Variety snippet; Deadline snippet; DGA; BAFTA; Polymarket

nominees: Come See Me in the Good Light
ballot.why: Vanity Fair's call lines up with the strongest late-season documentary resume: Critics Choice plus Independent Spirit. That makes The Perfect Neighbor the safest ballot answer even if docs can spring surprises.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Critics Choice; Independent Spirit; Polymarket

nominees: Armed Only with a Camera: The Life and Death of Brent Renaud
Children No More: 'Were and Are Gone'
ballot.challenger: Armed Only with a Camera: The Life and Death of Brent Renaud
ballot.why: Vanity Fair and Polymarket both put All the Empty Rooms in front. That's enough for a pool pick, but I still treat any short as medium-risk.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Polymarket

ballot.why: Editing is part of the One Battle after Another best-picture/directing package. BAFTA went there, and the market has widened the gap enough that I'd follow it.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; BAFTA; ACE context; Polymarket

ballot.why: This is a legit two-way split. Vanity Fair and BAFTA lean Sentimental Value, while The New Yorker and the Globe point to The Secret Agent. I give Sentimental Value a slight edge because the BAFTA plus market combo feels more Oscar-adjacent at the finish line.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; BAFTA; Golden Globes; LA Times snippet; Polymarket

ballot.why: Vanity Fair and Polymarket both shade this toward Two People Exchanging Saliva. That is enough for a pool answer, but the live-action short field is still close.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Polymarket

ballot.why: Frankenstein has the classic makeup branch profile and cleaned up the major precursor that matters most here. Easy checkbox.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; BAFTA; Polymarket

ballot.why: Score is one of the cleanest Sinners calls on the board: Globe, Critics Choice, BAFTA, Vanity Fair, and Polymarket all line up here.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Golden Globes; Critics Choice; BAFTA; Polymarket

ballot.why: Golden already won the Globe, gets the Vanity Fair pick, and Polymarket keeps it well ahead. I would not get cute here.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Golden Globes; Polymarket

ballot.why: This is another Frankenstein tech race. BAFTA and Vanity Fair both point there, and even Deadline's indexed snippet did too.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; Deadline snippet; BAFTA; Polymarket

ballot.why: F1 has the best sound-specific precursor on the board after taking the CAS top prize, and Vanity Fair also calls it. This feels sturdier than the picture titles in the category.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; CAS; MPSE context; Polymarket

ballot.why: Visual Effects is doing what this category usually does: follow the biggest technical branch signal. Avatar won the VES prize and gets Vanity Fair's vote too.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; VES; Polymarket

ballot.why: This race is basically the safest One Battle after Another checkmark: Critics Choice, WGA, BAFTA, Vanity Fair, and The New Yorker all have it.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Critics Choice; WGA; BAFTA; Polymarket

ballot.why: Original Screenplay has become the cleanest Sinners play: WGA, BAFTA, Vanity Fair, The New Yorker, and the market all align.
ballot.source_support: Vanity Fair; New Yorker; Critics Choice; WGA; BAFTA; Polymarket

extra_credit.note: ABC affiliates are scheduling the ceremony to hand off around 10:32 p.m. ET, which makes 3:32 my best tiebreaker guess. The official ceremony start is 7 p.m. ET / 4 p.m. PT.
Pure fashion-risk dart throw, not data. He is a likely attendee and one of the nominees most likely to take a big menswear swing.
